macro_segments.title: Introduction and Review of Previous Daf: The Need for a Pasuk to Exclude Ervah from Yibum

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and review of the two drashos explaining why a pasuk is needed for the case of ervah in yibum

macro_segments.title: Rabbi's Drasha from Velakach Veyibmah and the Derivation of Tzarat Ervah

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's reading of velakach veyibmah to exclude tzarot ervayot from the obligation of yibum
Gemara's challenge: where is tzara mentioned in the pasuk, and Rabbi's response using the extra hei

macro_segments.title: Introduction and Review of Previous Daf: The Need for a Pasuk to Exclude Ervah from Yibum (continued)

micro_segments.title: Explanation of zika (quasi-connection) and the concept of achos zekuka in the case of two brothers married to two sisters

macro_segments.title: Rabbi's Drasha from Velakach Veyibmah and the Derivation of Tzarat Ervah (continued)

micro_segments.title: How Rabbi derives tzara from velakcha: in cases of two potential marriages, there must be a choice
Viyibmah teaches that tzara is only an issue in the context of yibum, not outside of yibum

macro_segments.title: Cross-Examination of Drashos: How Rabbanan and Rabbi Explain Each Other's Pesukim

micro_segments.title: Rabbanan's use of the extra hei in velakcha to teach that yibum creates a normal marriage status allowing divorce and remarriage
Viyibmah teaches that yibum can occur even without the woman's consent, unlike regular kiddushin
Rabbi's challenge to Rabbanan: what does Rabbi do with the extra hei in velakcha and viyibmah
Rabbi derives from aleha that the category of sin requiring a chatat offering is limited to sins punishable by kares

macro_segments.title: The Category of Sins Requiring a Chatat: Derivation from Multiple Pesukim and Gezeirot Shavot

micro_segments.title: Teaching of the Baraita: sins requiring chatat are only those punishable by kares if done intentionally
Derivation for congregation in regular mitzvot using aleha from the pasuk regarding unknowing sin
Derivation for Kohen Gadol (Mashiach) through lashmat ha'am equating him with the congregation
Derivation for nasi and yachid (individuals) through mitzvot gezeira shava and the vav connecting cases
Rabbanan's source for this principle from the pasuk torah achas yihyeh lachem regarding avodah zarah

macro_segments.title: Rabbi's Application of Torah Achas and Distinction Between Individual and Congregation in Avodah Zarah

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's use of torah achas to derive that individual and congregation bring the same korban even for avodah zarah
Rebbi Chilkiya's challenge: why would we need torah achas if we would have anyway assumed different korbans
Gemara's response: the mitzvot listed would result in impossible korban combinations without torah achas

macro_segments.title: Return to Mishnah: The Missing Category of Mother and the Role of Machloket

micro_segments.title: Levi questions Rabbi: why does the mishnah list fifteen women instead of sixteen, with mother missing
Rabbi's response: mother who is not the father's wife (anusat aviv) is a matter of machloket between Rabbi Yehuda and Rabbanan
Gemara explains: the mishnah deals only with unanimous positions, not matters of debate
Gemara mentions Beis Shammai's position eliminating tzarot ervah entirely and explains why Beis Shammai is not considered

topical_tags.term: Achos Zekuka (sister of one to whom there is zika)
Chalitza (removing the shoe, alternative to yibum)
Torah Achas (one law applies to all)
Velakcha Veyibmah (take her and yibum her)
Anusat Aviv (woman his father raped, non-wife)
Rabbi Yehuda's stringency on father's concubine
Issur Kedusha (prohibition due to sanctity issues)
Rabbi Akiva's stringency on issur lav
